title: Vendingpreneurs
content:
What Would You Do?
Create
SHOWING EVERYTHING
 
SORTED BY LAST ACTIVITY
John Sherrill
3d ago
How should I respond to this?

This is my first "warm" lead from the platinum level process. It was texted to me like… day 3 or so of joining the group! Had a great pop in - but this was their response:

Good afternoon,

Sorry for the…continue

👍
❤️
💡
👏
😂
All Comments
3 Comments
Matthew Bacine
5h
John, I would follow up politely to inquire why they decided against the amenity so you can learn about their onjections a bit more. Then watch Anthony's follow up video (https://community.vendingpreneurs.com/posts/module-5-pop-ins-sales-foundations-what-to-say-in-a-follow-up) to learn how to do follow ups with them every couple of months. Adding value in your follow up  …more
👍
1
John Sherrill
2h

Thanks, bud.

Kylie Poon
28m

It's a polite way of them saying no, I usually will do another pop-in 6-9 months, there's a chance their ownership/pm change.

Sean, I personally do not like the idea of placing a machine when there are other machines in a location. Ideally I would recommend not going into a location unless there are no other operators with machines in a location, but again, depending on your comfort level  …more

⚡️

Yes. On a limited basis, but yes.
Kyle Davey
⚡️
4d
For a little more context, I feel like I have to offer fresh in my market because there’s so many competitors. I don’t really like it. It’s a headache logistically. But although margins may be smaller, profit per unit is generally higher, so it kind of works out. If I only sell three sandwiches, but they each profit four bucks, that’s probably the equivalent to …more
👍
1
DJ Fuchs
4d
I do not have the service fee. I also have fresh items on a limited basis, but start slow. Keep a smaller par level on those items and just be comfortable with running lower on those items as opposed to stocking too much and dealing with spoilage.
